paragraphs.text:
Independent literature sources
- SRD‑46 links 68 unique literature records (distinct citations) to stability constants for the Cd2+–chloride system (from 1930 to 1993) via the VLM entries we queried. Note: this count reflects all unique citations linked in SRD‑46; some may be methods/compilations in addition to primary measurements. A strict “primary only” tally would require per‑citation vetting.

paragraphs.text:
Stability-constant ranges and conditions (25 °C; aqueous; conditional on ionic strength)
- Species observed in this SRD‑46 selection: CdCl+ (ML), CdCl2(aq) (ML2), CdCl3− (ML3). No CdCl4^2− (ML4) measurements appear in the retrieved set (label present but 0 entries).
- Measurements are at 25 °C across ionic strength windows centered near 0, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, and 4 M (reference maps span ~20–30 °C overall, but the logged constants are at 25 °C).

paragraphs.text:
Notes and interpretation
- The constants are conditional and vary with ionic strength: for ML and ML2 the largest log β appears at I ≈ 0 M, while ML3 increases to its largest value at I ≈ 4 M. This reflects activity-coefficient and complexation effects in chloride medium; values are not directly comparable across different I without correction.
- Within this SRD‑46 subset there are 30 logK measurements at 25 °C covering the ionic‑strength windows listed above; thermodynamic ΔH and ΔS entries are also present but are not tabulated here as the question focuses on stability constants.
